For this presentation, I'll simplify that continuum into three practical positions, assist,
augment and automate.
I want to emphasize something here.
When we say assist, augment, automate, these are not just abstract categories.
They translate directly into technical design choices.
For example, assist means your AI generates an output, displays it to a user and stops.
The system does nothing else, think of it as a read-only recommendation.
Augment means the AI shapes the decision.
It pre-feels a form, suggests a classification, highlights a risk, but a human has to confirm
before anything happens.
There is a validation gate in the workflow.
Automate means the system is authorized to act.
The AI output triggers an API call without human intervention.
An issue is created, the workflow is started, a status is updated.
The technical difference between these three is not trivial.
It's the difference between a system that renders a dashboard and a system that writes
to your project database.
